A.rationale: Extends route() to (method,path) keying preserving @route pattern; fetch-then-delete returns err not raises. Convention-faithful, sound, sharp key-coercion probe.

B.rationale: Claims convention fidelity and is tightest, but bakes method into path string instead of real method dispatch; brittle routing if route() only keys on path.

C.rationale: Proper (method,path) tuple keying, existence-check then err, explicitly affirms err/ok+str.format conventions. Equivalent to A, slightly slower wall time.

D.rationale: Correct (method,path) keying with DB.pop, but adds unrequested get_item method=GET edit and frames error vaguely as 404-style; self-flagged race probe less central.

notes: A, C technically tied: both extend framework idiomatically with err-not-raise. C names conventions more explicitly; A's key-coercion probe edges it. B under-engineers routing via path hack; D mild scope creep plus vaguer convention adherence.
